events.description: Dr. David Dao is forcibly removed from United Express Flight 3411 by aviation security officers after refusing to give up his seat for airline crew.
Videos of the violent removal go viral on social media, sparking global outrage and widespread condemnation of United Airlines' handling of the situation.
United Airlines CEO Oscar Munoz issues an initial statement apologizing for having to 're-accommodate' customers, which is widely criticized as tone-deaf.
United Airlines stock price drops significantly following the public relations fallout, wiping out nearly $1 billion in market value.
CEO Oscar Munoz issues a second, more contrite apology, taking full responsibility and promising a thorough review of airline policies.
United Airlines reaches an undisclosed settlement with Dr. David Dao to resolve claims related to the incident.
United Airlines announces a new policy allowing passengers to receive up to $10,000 in compensation for voluntarily giving up their seats.

stakeholders.name: Oscar Munoz
stakeholders.position: Initially defended the airline's actions by calling the passenger 'disruptive and belligerent,' but later issued a formal apology and promised a full review of company policies.

stakeholders.name: David Dao
stakeholders.position: Through his legal counsel, he expressed that he was 'traumatized' by the incident and sought to hold the airline and authorities accountable for his injuries.
stakeholders.source: Press conference held by his legal team

stakeholders.name: Thomas Demetrio
stakeholders.position: Argued that the airline's actions were a clear case of excessive force and that the incident highlighted a systemic issue with how airlines treat their customers.

stakeholders.name: Sean Spicer
stakeholders.position: Stated that the incident was 'troubling' and that the President had been briefed on the situation, noting that it was not the way any passenger should be treated.

stakeholders.name: Rahm Emanuel
stakeholders.position: Called the incident 'completely unacceptable' and demanded a thorough investigation into the conduct of the aviation security officers involved.

outcome_description: The incident triggered a massive global public relations crisis for United Airlines, resulting in widespread condemnation, a significant drop in brand sentiment, and a subsequent overhaul of the airline's passenger handling and overbooking policies. The airline reached an undisclosed financial settlement with Dr. David Dao shortly after the incident.
outcome_source: Confidence < 0.5; no singular quantitative survey exists, but sentiment analysis from the period (e.g., YouGov BrandIndex) showed United's brand perception score plummeted to its lowest level in years following the viral video.

key_figures.injuries_sustained: broken nose, concussion, and loss of two teeth
